name: Neil's Burn Lisa's Mouth Chicken Nuggets
recipeInstructions:
No mess. No cleaning. 15-20 minute special.
Defrost chicken. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Line a baking tray with tinfoil and grease with butter. (Butter goes back in fridge now.)
Cut chicken into 1-inch cubes, or whatever shape looks about right.
Dump breadcrumbs into a large freezer bag.
Add Cajun spices to the bag. Add red pepper flakes. Toss in a little white pepper and add more red pepper flakes. Add some black pepper and white pepper in an amount that looks pretty. Add more red pepper flakes and disguise them with paprika. Add more Cajun spices to cover up any mess of red pepper. Add more breadcrumbs and shake until well mixed.
Toss all chicken pieces into the freezer bag.
Dance around the kitchen singing the dead chicken song. Shake the bag during the refrain.
Unload half the chicken pieces from the bag, keeping only those that are breaded. Place them on the tray in a single layer.
Play the dead chicken song again and shake more vigorously. (For extra fun, add more red pepper flakes.)
Add the remaining chicken pieces to the tray, arranging them in small rows. If any pieces pile up, remove the offending piece, beat it with a mallet, and place it back on the tray, preferably in the corner.
Place the tray in the oven. The oven should be warm by now. Set a timer for 40 minutes and leave.
After 15 minutes, return and remove the chicken. Serve dinner with Coke, preferably flat. When the alarm goes off (timer), dinner is done.
Dispose of paper plates and foil from the tray. Put all utensils away—they aren't dirty.
